keyEvents.impact: Deep historical grievance toward Japan that still complicates relations with a natural security partner
Peninsula divided at the DMZ; permanent existential threat from the North and reliance on US security guarantee
Miracle on the Han River: chaebol-driven growth transformed a war-ruined country into an industrial power
Mass protests ended military rule; direct presidential elections and a vibrant, contentious democracy

keyEvents.event: Asian financial crisis and IMF bailout
keyEvents.impact: National humiliation followed by reform; lasting sensitivity about economic sovereignty and chaebol power
North Korean provocations killed dozens; hardened deterrence posture and conventional retaliation doctrine
Candlelight protests demonstrated democratic resilience but deepened partisan polarization

history.narrative: A trading powerhouse living under the artillery of a nuclear-armed neighbor, South Korea anchors its security in the US alliance while hedging economically with China. Its relationship with Japan is complicated by colonial memory despite shared threats. Strategy is fundamentally defensive: deter the North, keep export markets open, and avoid being trampled in great-power competition.

redLines.description: North Korean armed attack on South Korean territory or vessels
North Korean nuclear use or imminent nuclear strike preparations
Withdrawal of US forces or abandonment of the mutual defense treaty
Blockade of sea lanes carrying Korean trade and energy imports

internalDivisions.name: Progressive-conservative divide over North Korea policy
Generational and gender divides over economy and conscription

goals.description: Deter North Korean aggression and survive its provocations without escalation to war
Keep the US alliance and extended nuclear deterrence credible
Maintain leadership in semiconductors, shipbuilding, and high-tech exports
Expand global influence as a middle power through trade, culture, and defense exports
